perturbation.original_text: Obligatio ns of parties under this Ag reemen t shall not appl y to i nform ation which: a. was alread y in the publi c domain when disclosed; or sub sequent ly falls into t he public d omain other than throug h disclosure by the Recipient; or disclosed by the R ecipient with the written approval of the Owner; b. was alread y in the possession of or known by the Recipient free of any obligation at the time disclosed to Recipient; c. beco mes known to the Recipient from a source o ther than the Owner with out breach of this Agreement by the Recipient; d. is develope d independen tly by the Recipient withou t use of the Owner’s Propri etary Inform ation; or e. is disclosed p ursuant t o judicial or governm ental order, provided that t he Recipient notifies the Owner so that an adequate opp ortunit y is gi ven to respon d to such order.
perturbation.changed_text: Obligatio ns of parties under this Ag reemen t shall not appl y to i nform ation which: a. was alread y in the publi c domain when disclosed; or sub sequent ly falls into t he public d omain other than throug h disclosure by the Recipient; or disclosed by the R ecipient with the written approval of the Transferor; b. was alread y in the possession of or known by the Recipient free of any obligation at the time disclosed to Recipient; c. beco mes known to the Recipient from a source o ther than the Owner with out breach of this Agreement by the Recipient; d. is develope d independen tly by the Recipient withou t use of the Owner’s Propri etary Inform ation; or e. is disclosed p ursuant t o judicial or governm ental order, provided that t he Recipient notifies the Owner so that an adequate opp ortunit y is gi ven to respon d to such order.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Owner' to 'Transferor' in subsection (a) but keeping 'Owner' in subsections (c), (d) and (e) creates ambiguity. It's now unclear to whom the Recipient must get written approval from, if it's the 'Transferor' or 'Owner,' if these are in fact supposed to represent the same entity. Additionally, regarding subsection (c) and (d), it is unclear from whom the recipient is getting Proprietary Information and whose Proprietary Information should not be used. This internal inconsistency undermines the clarity of the exceptions to the confidentiality obligations, since there are multiple references within the same section, this makes this an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obligatio ns of parties under this Ag reemen t shall not appl y to i nform ation which: a. was alread y in the publi c domain when disclosed; or sub sequent ly falls into t he public d omain other than throug h disclosure by the Recipient; or disclosed by the R ecipient with the written approval of the Owner; b. was alread y in the possession of or known by the Recipient free of any obligation at the time disclosed to Recipient; c. beco mes known to the Recipient from a source o ther than the Owner with out breach of this Agreement by the Recipient; d. is develope d independen tly by the Recipient withou t use of the Owner’s Propri etary Inform ation; or e. is disclosed p ursuant t o judicial or governm ental order, provided that t he Recipient notifies the Owner so that an adequate opp ortunit y is gi ven to respon d to such order.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Owner' with 'Transferor' in subsection (a) of the Obligations section, while subsections (c), (d) and (e) still refer to the 'Owner'. The contradicted text (which is the original text) only refers to the 'Owner'. This creates a direct conflict because it's unclear from whom the Recipient needs written approval, or from whom the recipient is getting Proprietary Information and whose Proprietary Information should not be used, thus making the document inconsistent and adhering to the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, since the interchangeable use of 'Owner' and 'Transferor' without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.explanation: Changing 'parties' to 'signatories' implies something different. The term 'signatories' refers to those who signed the contract initially, this change conflicts with the general understanding that amendments may involve different or additional parties, especially if the original parties assign their rights. By restricting modifications to only 'signatories,' the contract introduces an in-text contradiction by limiting the scope of potential modifications and parties involved, since at the bottom it still refers to Parties in question to Name, Title and Date.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies "parties" to "signatories." According to the provided explanation, this creates an in-text contradiction with the original statement regarding who can modify the agreement, as it limits potential modifications and parties involved, contradicting the 'parties' stated in the Contradicted Text for Name, Title, and Date. The Contradicted text is identical to the original text, and this change impacts the scope of the agreement, creating a direct conflict that makes the term of who can modify the agreement ambiguious.

perturbation.original_text: For Proprietary Information receiv ed from the disclosing party, the Recipient agrees to: a. hold it in confidence w ith the sa me degree of c are w ith which it protects its own confidential and proprietary inform ation, but at least re asonable care ; b. restrict dis closure of it only to its e mployees, agents and contractors with a need to know basis and advise thos e persons of their obligations to keep it confidential; c. except for the purposes of this Agre ement, not copy or duplicate, or knowingl y allow anyone else to copy or duplicate it; d. not publish, disclose or make availab le any benchmark or testing results without the written consent of both parties; and e. on req uest or up on term ination of this Agreem ent, prom ptly return all Proprietary Inform ation in a tangible form or certify that such Proprietary Inform ation is destroy ed.
perturbation.changed_text: For Proprietary Information receiv ed from the revealing party, the Recipient agrees to: a. hold it in confidence w ith the sa me degree of c are w ith which it protects its own confidential and proprietary inform ation, but at least re asonable care ; b. restrict dis closure of it only to its e mployees, agents and contractors with a need to know basis and advise thos e persons of their obligations to keep it confidential; c. except for the purposes of this Agre ement, not copy or duplicate, or knowingl y allow anyone else to copy or duplicate it; d. not publish, disclose or make availab le any benchmark or testing results without the written consent of both parties; and e. on req uest or up on term ination of this Agreem ent, prom ptly return all Proprietary Inform ation in a tangible form or certify that such Proprietary Inform ation is destroy ed.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'disclosing party' to 'revealing party' does not seem like a contradiction, however the term 'disclosing party' is not defined. It causes uncertainty about who the 'revealing party' is in relation to the rest of the agreement, especially since the term revealing party is not used in the definition of 'Owner' or anywhere else. While the sentence 'not publish, disclose or make available any benchmark or testing results without the written consent of both parties' makes reference to 'both parties' this doesn't clarify what the revealing party is in relation to the parties of this agreement, since this change of terminology in the first sentence of this section creates this in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: For Proprietary Information receiv ed from the disclosing party, the Recipient agrees to: a. hold it in confidence w ith the sa me degree of c are w ith which it protects its own confidential and proprietary inform ation, but at least re asonable care ; b. restrict dis closure of it only to its e mployees, agents and contractors with a need to know basis and advise thos e persons of their obligations to keep it confidential; c. except for the purposes of this Agre ement, not copy or duplicate, or knowingl y allow anyone else to copy or duplicate it; d. not publish, disclose or make availab le any benchmark or testing results without the written consent of both parties; and e. on req uest or up on term ination of this Agreem ent, prom ptly return all Proprietary Inform ation in a tangible form or certify that such Proprietary Inform ation is destroy ed.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'disclosing party' to 'revealing party' introduces a misaligned terminology that creates an in-text contradiction. The term 'disclosing party' while not defined, is used consistently in the original text. By changing it to 'revealing party,' and because this term is not defined anywhere in the document, it creates uncertainty about who the 'revealing party' is in relation to the other parties involved, particularly since the 'Owner' definition and other references do not use this term. The Contradicted Text is identical to the Changed Text, however, it is the introduction of the change that creates the contradiction based on the explanation given and the definition for misaligned terminology.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees to main tain the confidentiality of such Pr oprietary Inform ation and to not use, disclose, publish or dissem inate it in an y manner not consiste nt with this Agreement. In additi on to any other remedies, which may be available, Owner shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief to enforce the ter ms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each signatory agrees to main tain the confidentiality of such Pr oprietary Inform ation and to not use, disclose, publish or dissem inate it in an y manner not consiste nt with this Agreement. In additi on to any other remedies, which may be available, Owner shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief to enforce the ter ms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'party' to 'signatory' in the first sentence, while the second sentence mentions the 'Owner,' creates an inconsistency. If 'signatory' refers only to the parties who signed the agreement initially and doesn't encompass assignees or successors, it raises a contradiction. Specifically, if the Owner of the information is not the same as the original signatory due to assignment, it becomes unclear whether the Owner's rights to injunctive relief are affected, because the original signatory is obligated to not disclose the proprietary information, however the owner is granted the right to seek injunctive relief, however both parties are now being referred to as two different things. This undermines clarity regarding enforcement rights, therefore it is an in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees to main tain the confidentiality of such Pr oprietary Inform ation and to not use, disclose, publish or dissem inate it in an y manner not consiste nt with this Agreement. In additi on to any other remedies, which may be available, Owner shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief to enforce the ter ms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'party' to 'signatory' introduces a potential contradiction because 'signatory' may be interpreted more narrowly than 'party,' potentially excluding assignees or successors of the original signatories. The original text uses 'party' in both the original clause and the contradicted clause, implying a broader scope of responsibility and entitlement. Since the term 'owner' is used later in the same sentence and refers to the right to seek injunctive relief, limiting the first part of the sentence to only signatories could conflict with the owner's ability to seek relief if the owner is not an original signatory. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology, which states that using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations, therefore it is a strong in-text contradiction.
